title: Russian Judge Verdict
description: Schema for a Russian Judge verdict produced by an adversarial reviewer. Mirrors the JSON form documented in templates/verdict-template.md and the field rules in PROTOCOL.md §7. The five required fields are the core verdict; the optional `record` block (PROTOCOL.md §13) carries the auditable-record metadata that programmatic pipelines use to chain, bind, and attribute verdicts.

round.description: Review round identifier. R1, R2, R3, etc.

modality.description: Review modality. Per PROTOCOL.md §9. Dual indicates two independent verdicts (one Code, one Domain) rolled up; in practice the two are usually emitted as separate verdicts, each with its own modality.

score.description: Reviewer score in [0.0, 10.0]. By convention one decimal of precision; not machine-enforced, because a multipleOf 0.1 constraint spuriously rejects exact one-decimal values such as 9.1 and 9.2 under IEEE-754 float. Anchored bands per PROTOCOL.md §4.1.

important.description: Important findings. Block shipping at the pass floor.

minor.description: Minor findings. Do not block shipping.

notes.description: Optional. Notes are observations with no score or floor impact: out-of-scope remarks, no-action recommendations, sibling-class observations, future-improvement framing. Per PROTOCOL.md §3.6, a finding whose own narrative says 'no fix required here' or 'out of scope' belongs in Notes, not Minor. Notes do not count toward the floor.

verdict.description: Computed verdict value. PASS requires score>=9.0 AND 0 critical AND 0 important. REVISE is the default below the floor. REWORK is reserved for score<7.0. Validators should verify the verdict is consistent with the score and finding counts.

record.description: Optional auditable-record metadata, per PROTOCOL.md §13. Present when the verdict is stored as a durable, attributable, chainable record (audit trail, programmatic pipeline) rather than an ephemeral message. Omit for ad-hoc single-session use.

verdict_id.description: Stable identifier for this verdict, unique within the verdict store (e.g. a UUID). Referenced by `predecessor_verdict` to chain rounds.

predecessor_verdict.description: The `verdict_id` of the preceding round's verdict, or null for R1. Chains the round protocol (PROTOCOL.md §5) into a verifiable lineage rather than relying on round numbers alone.

review_scope.description: What the reviewer actually examined, per modality. Makes a PASS honest: it certifies only this scope, not adjacent files or downstream consumers (PROTOCOL.md §4.2.1). At least one dimension list is present; the off-modality list is an explicit empty array, never omitted.

code_dimensions.description: Code axes reviewed (e.g. 'edge cases', 'contract conformance'). Empty array for a domain-only review.

domain_dimensions.description: Domain/content axes reviewed (e.g. 'citation accuracy', 'internal consistency'). Empty array for a code-only review.

covers_range.description: The exact change the verdict is bound to (e.g. a commit range `<base>..<head>`, a revision id, or a content hash). A verdict authored against one state does not certify a later state; binding the verdict to a range is what lets tooling gate the *shipped* change rather than a stale one.

reviewer_model.description: Identifier of the model (or human) that produced this verdict. Required for cross-model dispatch (PROTOCOL.md §9.4) to be auditable - you cannot tell which model caught a finding without recording which model reviewed.

review_round.description: Round identifier, mirroring the top-level `round`. Carried in the record so the stored verdict is self-describing.

pass_asserted.description: The reviewer's own PASS assertion. Under the default floor (PROTOCOL.md §4.2) it MUST equal `(score >= 9.0) AND (critical == 0) AND (important == 0)`; Minor findings do not affect the default floor. A `pass_asserted: true` with any non-zero Critical or Important count is a malformed verdict; validators recompute and reject the mismatch. Stricter floors are selected by `floor_variant` (PROTOCOL.md §4.5): the Minor-inclusive variant additionally requires 0 Minor, and the Relaxed-Minor variant lowers the score bar to 8.5.

floor_variant.description: Which pass-floor variant (PROTOCOL.md §4.5) governs `pass_asserted`. Absent or `default`: score >= 9.0 AND 0 Critical AND 0 Important, Minors permitted. `minor_inclusive`: additionally 0 Minor. `relaxed_minor`: score >= 8.5 AND 0 Critical AND 0 Important, one Minor permitted. Declarative; the schema recomputes the matching floor for `pass_asserted`.

issued_at.description: Timestamp the verdict was issued (ISO 8601 UTC recommended). Orders the round lineage and supports drift auditing (PROTOCOL.md §10.2).

allOf.$comment: C-1 floor enforcement. Default floor (PROTOCOL.md §4.2): when a record asserts pass_asserted:true and no stricter floor_variant is selected, require score >= 9.0 and zero Critical and zero Important. Minor findings do NOT affect the default floor.
Minor-inclusive variant (PROTOCOL.md §4.5): pass_asserted:true additionally requires zero Minor.
Relaxed-Minor variant (PROTOCOL.md §4.5): score bar 8.5, zero Critical and zero Important, one Minor permitted.

id.description: Finding identifier. Class letter (C or I) + dash + 1-indexed integer, monotonically increasing within the class.

description.description: One-sentence description of the defect, ending with a period.

impact.description: One-sentence description of the consequence if shipped, ending with a period. Required for Critical and Important.

id.description: Minor finding identifier. M-1, M-2, etc.

description.description: One-sentence description of the defect, ending with a period.

impact.description: Optional one-sentence consequence statement. Not required for Minor.

description.description: One-sentence observation. Notes carry no score or floor impact (PROTOCOL.md §3.6).
